title: Convert Decimal to Binary

content.problemStatement:
Let's implement a stack using arrays to solve a classic problem: converting an integer from decimal to binary using the "divisor-remainder" method.

In this method, you repeatedly divide the decimal number by 2 and keep track of the remainders. 

The remainders, when read from bottom to top, give you the binary representation of the number.

Update the code given in the IDE to solve this problem.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing the binary representation of N without leading zeros. The number 0 is represented as "0".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 10 → 1010, 15 → 1111, 18 → 10010 using repeated division by 2 and reading remainders in reverse order.
Zero is a special case and is represented by a single "0".

solution.approach: We repeatedly divide the given number by 2 and store the remainders (0 or 1) on a stack (or a dynamic array). The binary representation is obtained by reading the remainders in reverse order, i.e., by popping the stack. This is exactly the classic divisor‑remainder method. For each test case we perform at most ⌊log2 N⌋+1 divisions, which is fast enough for the given limits. The algorithm uses only O(log N) auxiliary space for the stack of bits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log N) where N is the value of each test case (at most 30 operations per test case because N ≤ 10^9).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log N) auxiliary space for the bits of a single number.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(0)
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1)
    elif i == 2:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(2)
    elif i == 3:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(3)
    elif i == 4:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1000000000)
    elif i == 5:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999999999)
    elif i == 6:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print(1000000000)
        print(1000000000)
    elif i == 7:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(0)
        print(1000000000)
        print(1)
    elif i == 8:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for _ in range(t):
            print(7)
    elif i == 9:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        for _ in range(t):
            print(15)
    elif i == 10:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for n in range(1,6):
            print(n)
    elif i == 11:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for n in range(5,0,-1):
            print(n)
    elif i == 12:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999999937)
    elif i == 13:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(POWER(2,29))
    elif i == 14:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(POWER(2,29)-1)
    elif i == 15:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(123456789)
    elif i == 16:
        t = 10
        print(t)
        for n in range(10):
            print(n)
    elif i == 17:
        t = 20
        print(t)
        for n in range(20):
            print(n)
    elif i == 18:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(1000000000)
        print(999999999)
        print(POWER(2,29))
    elif i == 19:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        print(0)
        print(1000000000)
        print(0)
        print(1000000000)
    else:
        # default fallback
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(0)
